Calculation.Text: "View all media with subtitles in title"

ScriptReference.name: Go all Block instances in Title

StyledText.Data: The name of the building block should be a descriptive name that gives a hint of the purpose of the block. Ex. “Chapter navigation button” or “Page headline”. The name will be JSON-ified in the reference. WARNING! This name should not be changed without informing the developers, since it is referenced in the code.

CharacterStyle.Text: WARNING! This name should not be changed without informing the developers, since it is referenced in the code.

StyledText.Data: The description of the building block can be viewed by all roles except Translators.
If the Block is based on a template, the name is shown here.

Calculation.Text: "Type a label name for this textfield"

StyledText.Data: This is the text on the layout where the editor should enter the text. Ex. “Name of job role”, “Plain text”. WARNING! Label names should not be changed without informing the developers, since they are referenced in the code.

CharacterStyle.Text: WARNING! Label names should not be changed without informing the developers, since they are referenced in the code.

StyledText.Data: This number defines the maximum of characters the editor or translator is allowed to enter inte the text field. Leave blank for no limit.

Calculation.Text: FAT Block::Number of text fields <1 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id ≠ ""

StyledText.Data: If this option is checked, a formatting bar will be visible above the text input field. The formatting bar is only visible in panels including “html” in their name. This option can be changed at any time during development.

Calculation.Text: "Type a label name for this textfield"
FAT Block::Number of text fields <2 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id ≠ ""

StyledText.Data: If this option is checked, the text entered in the page item will be directly accessible in the page item JSON and not referenced via a text id in the text JSON file. The text in the JSON will be URL encoded. This can be useful when entering a URL or a value in the textfield. This option can be changed at any time during development.

Calculation.Text: "Type a label name for this textfield"
FAT Block::Number of text fields <3 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id ≠ ""
"Type a label name for this textfield"
FAT Block::Number of text fields <4 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id ≠ ""
"Type a label name for this textfield"
FAT Block::Number of text fields <5 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id ≠ ""
"Type a label name for this textfield"
FAT Block::Number of text fields <6 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id ≠ ""
"Type a label name for this textfield"
FAT Block::Number of text fields <7 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id ≠ ""
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <1 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id = ""
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <2 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id = ""
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <3 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id = ""
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <4 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id = ""
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <5 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id = ""
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <6 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id = ""
BlockTemplate::Number of text fields <7 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id = ""
"Type a label name for this mediafield"
"Type a label name for this mediafield"

StyledText.Data: If this option is checked, the media card window in FAT will enable settings for: - Selecting a Media IO Warning: It will also change the Media key in the Page item JSON from a String to a JSONObject.

Calculation.Text: "Type a name for the content array header"

StyledText.Data: This is the visible text label on the layout where the editor should enter text or media. Ex. “Option”, “Feedback”. WARNING! Label names should not be changed without informing the developers, since they are referenced in the code.

CharacterStyle.Text: WARNING! Label names should not be changed without informing the developers, since they are referenced in the code.

StyledText.Data: The Value is saved in the PageItem JSON and used for different purposes - if an alternative answer is correct, the sort order of a drag and drop activity and similar. Enter what kind of value type you want the user to enter - Boolean (dropdown menu, true/false), Number, String or None.

Calculation.Text: FAT Block::List number of value fields = 0
FAT Block::List number of media containers = 0
FAT Block::List number of media containers = 0

StyledText.Data: A content array is an array of items. Each item can contain textfields, media containers and value fields. The number of items can vary between different page items built with the same symbol.. Content arrays are used when creating options for a question for example. Here are some typical content arrays: Radio button options in text 1 Text field. Label name “Option” 1 Value field, Type = Boolean. Label name “Correct option” (= true, otherwise false) Sort images 1 Media container. Label name “Image” 1 Value field, Type = Number. Label name “Sort order” Checkbox question with feedback 2 Text fields. Label name “Option” and “Feedback” 1 Value field, Type = Boolean. Label name “Correct option” (= true, otherwise false) Select how many of each field type you want your content array to contain.

ValueListReference.name: Number of Media container overrides List

Calculation.Text: FAT Block::List number of value fields = 0
FAT Block::List number of media containers = 0
FAT Block::List number of value fields = 0
FAT Block::List number of media containers = 0

StyledText.Data: A content array is an array of items. The min and max items numbers sets limits for the authoring popup menu when creating a page item from the symbol. Example: Settting Min. items = 3 and Max. items = 6. Author popup up menu contain the numbers 3, 4, 5 and 6.

Calculation.Text: FAT Block::List number of value fields = 0 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id ≠ ""
FAT Block::List number of value fields = 0 Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id = ""
"Type a name for the content array header"

StyledText.Data: Add a specific attribute that should be added by the Author while authoring the learning programme. The attributes will be included in the page item JSON object only for this specifc type of Block.

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "level" ; "Block") & SetProperty ( "learningprogramme_id" ; FAT Title::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "reset_global_fields" ; True)
IsEmpty ( FAT Title::gModifierName ) Or IsEmpty ( FAT Title::gModifierType ) Or FAT Block::BlockTemplate id ≠ ""

StyledText.Data: You can choose any name for your attribute. However, the name will be “prettified” using _underscore_in the JSON key.

Choose attribute type depending on how you want to the JSON key value to be formatted. Prettified string Will replace capital letters with lower-case, åäö with aao and spaces with dash “-”. Number An integer value. If empty it will be replaced with 0 (zero). URL encoded string A string where all characters except A-Z, a-z are replaced with %. Media reference Will enable a media container as an attribute input. The value will be the prettified file name of the media file in the container. The media itself will be included in the media export. String Unaltered key value. Depending on characters, this might create a corrupt JSON. CSS Key values with type CSS will be exported to file “src/assets/css/cmt.scss”. The CSS key value can only be used for the Learning programme level.

CharacterStyle.Text: Key values with type CSS will be exported to file “src/assets/css/cmt.scss”. The CSS key value can only be used for the Learning programme level.

StyledText.Data: The attribute description will be visible for authors and editors when adding content and entering key values to the CMT.
This is the default value when creating a new page item based on this building block. The default value must be a valid value.
All global texts attached to this block
This building block is not based on a template.
